What They Make or Do: Northrop Grumman’s Sunnyvale Marine Systems operation designs and manufactures major naval propulsion and electric-power machinery, including ship-service turbine-generator equipment for nuclear submarines.
Primary Products or Capabilities: Ship-service turbine generators; turbine-generator sets; naval propulsion and power-generation machinery; associated engineering, production, testing and lifecycle support.
Operating Entity / Site Note: CAGE 90099 is associated with the Sunnyvale, California Marine Systems operation; this is a specialized operating site within the named legal entity, not the whole Northrop Grumman enterprise.
NAICS Assessment: Partially correct. NAICS 335312 captures motor/generator manufacturing, but understates the site’s role as an OEM of complete naval turbine-generator and propulsion machinery.
Verification Notes / Uncertainty: Public sources clearly establish the operating site and naval-power role; the profile does not infer which individual subaward line bought which assembly.

Primary NAICS Description: Power, Distribution, and Specialty Transformer Manufacturing
What They Make or Do: Leonardo DRS Naval Power Systems in Menomonee Falls engineers and manufactures shipboard electrical power-distribution, power-conversion and electric-propulsion equipment for U.S. Navy surface ships and submarines.
Primary Products or Capabilities: Marine switchboards and load centers; motor controllers and variable-frequency drives; power-conversion and distribution equipment; integrated electric-propulsion equipment.
Operating Entity / Site Note: The UEI is the Menomonee Falls, Wisconsin Naval Power Systems operating entity, historically DRS Power & Control Technologies.
NAICS Assessment: Misleading as primary. NAICS 335311 is transformer manufacturing; transformers are relevant, but the core offering is integrated switchgear, motor control, power conversion and propulsion equipment. The entity’s additional 335313/335314 codes are more representative.
Verification Notes / Uncertainty: Operating identity, products and ownership are documented on Leonardo DRS pages.

What They Make or Do: The legacy Rolls-Royce Marine North America naval-propulsors operation in Walpole, Massachusetts manufactured and supported controllable-pitch propellers and related propulsion hardware for Navy surface ships, including DDG-51. Its naval propulsors and handling assets transferred to Fairbanks Morse Defense in July 2025.
Primary Products or Capabilities: Controllable-pitch propeller systems; propeller hubs and blades; oil-distribution boxes; naval propulsors; repair and lifecycle support.
Verified Corporate Parent: Fairbanks Morse Defense (operating assets; legacy Rolls-Royce UEI/name may remain in historical data)
Operating Entity / Site Note: The row represents a legacy legal/registration identity. Rolls-Royce completed the sale of the Walpole and related naval-propulsors business to Fairbanks Morse Defense on July 16, 2025; current operating identity should be reviewed before using the UEI for outreach.
NAICS Assessment: Missing. No primary NAICS is resolved in the file. The demonstrated activity is marine propulsion-equipment manufacturing and support, not a generic Rolls-Royce corporate activity.
Domestic or Foreign: Domestic U.S. operation; U.S.-owned after the 2025 asset sale
Verification Notes / Uncertainty: High confidence in products and asset ownership; medium confidence that the legacy UEI remains the current contracting identity after the asset transfer.

Primary NAICS Description: Measuring, Dispensing, and Other Pumping Equipment Manufacturing
What They Make or Do: Curtiss-Wright’s Engineered Pump Division in Bethlehem, Pennsylvania designs, manufactures, tests and supports highly engineered pumps and pump components for U.S. Navy surface ships and submarines, including nuclear-service applications.
Primary Products or Capabilities: Critical-service centrifugal pumps; nuclear-qualified naval pumps; pump assemblies and components; impellers; spares, overhaul and test services.
Operating Entity / Site Note: CAGE 0UBG9 identifies the Bethlehem Engineered Pump Division. It is distinct from the Curtiss-Wright Electro-Mechanical Division row at Cheswick (CAGE 04808).
NAICS Assessment: Correct. NAICS 333914 is a good match for the division’s pump-manufacturing activity.
Verification Notes / Uncertainty: Operating site, product scope and Navy application are directly documented.

What They Make or Do: Scot Forge produces large open-die and rolled-ring forgings for marine propulsion and shipbuilding, supplied as forged, rough-machined or finish-machined parts.
Primary Products or Capabilities: Propeller, tail, main and intermediate shafts; rudder stocks and tubes; stern tubes; shaft couplings; gear blanks; large rolled rings and open-die forgings.
Operating Entity / Site Note: The UEI represents the U.S. forging company rather than a diversified parent. Scot Forge identifies its workforce as employee-owners.
NAICS Assessment: Correct. NAICS 332111 accurately describes iron and steel forging, although the code alone does not show the company’s marine-propulsion specialization or machining capability.
Verification Notes / Uncertainty: The company directly publishes the shipbuilding and propulsion product list.

What They Make or Do: BAE Systems’ U.S. naval-structures operations manufacture large, complex welded submarine structures and propulsion-related modules rather than complete ships.
Primary Products or Capabilities: Virginia Payload Module missile tubes; Virginia-class propulsors; Columbia-class bearing-support structures; large precision-welded submarine modules and fabrications.
Verified Ultimate Owner: BAE Systems plc (United Kingdom; public company)
Operating Entity / Site Note: The legal entity is BAE Systems Land & Armaments L.P., but the work profiled is its specialized U.S. naval/submarine manufacturing operation.
NAICS Assessment: Misleading if read literally. NAICS 336611 suggests whole-ship building and repair, while this entity is principally a first-tier maker of large submarine components and modules.
Domestic or Foreign: Domestic U.S. operating entity; foreign-owned (United Kingdom)
Verification Notes / Uncertainty: First-party releases identify the exact submarine structures and facilities.

Primary NAICS Description: Aircraft Engine and Engine Parts Manufacturing
What They Make or Do: GE Aerospace’s marine business manufactures LM2500 gas-turbine propulsion modules and related controls/support for DDG-51 destroyers.
Primary Products or Capabilities: LM2500 marine gas turbines; packaged gas-turbine modules; propulsion controls; spares, depot and lifecycle engineering support.
Verified Corporate Parent: GE Aerospace (business and brand of General Electric Company)
Operating Entity / Site Note: CAGE 07482 is associated with GE’s Evendale/Cincinnati engine operations; the Navy deliverable is the marine adaptation of an aeroderivative gas turbine.
NAICS Assessment: Misleading for this use. NAICS 336412 is accurate for GE’s aircraft-engine legal operation, but it obscures that this subaward supplier delivers marine gas-turbine propulsion modules for destroyers.
Verification Notes / Uncertainty: GE first-party sources directly link LM2500 modules to DDG-51.

Primary NAICS Description: Air-Conditioning and Warm Air Heating Equipment and Commercial and Industrial Refrigeration Equipment Manufacturing
What They Make or Do: Johnson Controls Navy Systems engineers and manufactures custom shipboard heating, ventilation, air-conditioning and refrigeration machinery for U.S. Navy submarines and surface ships.
Primary Products or Capabilities: YORK shipboard chillers and compressors; air-conditioning plants; condensers; refrigeration machinery; environmental-control systems and lifecycle support.
Verified Corporate Parent: Johnson Controls Federal Systems, LLC / Johnson Controls Navy Systems
Verified Ultimate Owner: Johnson Controls International plc (Ireland; public company)
Operating Entity / Site Note: The UEI is the dedicated Navy-systems operating company, not Johnson Controls’ commercial building-services business.
NAICS Assessment: Correct. NAICS 333415 accurately describes the core HVAC and refrigeration equipment, though it does not convey Navy qualification and system integration.
Verification Notes / Uncertainty: Johnson Controls’ Navy and marine pages identify the product scope and dedicated operating group.

Primary NAICS Description: Power Boiler and Heat Exchanger Manufacturing
What They Make or Do: BWXT Nuclear Operations Group manufactures nuclear-reactor components, fuel and major propulsion-plant assemblies for U.S. Navy submarines and aircraft carriers.
Primary Products or Capabilities: Naval nuclear reactor components; reactor fuel; heavy nuclear components and assemblies; engineering, manufacturing, inspection and lifecycle support.
Operating Entity / Site Note: This UEI is an operating nuclear-manufacturing group within BWXT and should not be confused with the separate BWX Technologies corporate-office UEI at rank 50.
NAICS Assessment: Partially correct. NAICS 332410 can cover boilers and heat exchangers, but is too narrow for the entity’s broader naval-reactor, fuel and nuclear-component mission.
Verification Notes / Uncertainty: BWXT directly describes its naval nuclear propulsion products and contracts.

What They Make or Do: D.C. Fabricators designs and manufactures custom condensers, heat exchangers, pressure equipment and complex corrosion-resistant fabrications for naval and nuclear applications.
Primary Products or Capabilities: Main and auxiliary condensers; shell-and-tube heat exchangers; pressure vessels; titanium, copper-nickel and dissimilar-metal weldments; machining, NDE and testing.
Verified Ultimate Owner: Privately held Precision Components Group; BWXT acquisition announced but not closed as of June 18, 2026
Operating Entity / Site Note: D.C. Fabricators is a specialized Florence, New Jersey operating company. BWXT announced an agreement to acquire its parent in April 2026, with closing expected in the second half of 2026; ownership is therefore recorded on an as-of-date basis.
NAICS Assessment: Misleading. NAICS 332312 (fabricated structural metal) does not capture the company’s main identity as a custom heat-exchanger, condenser and pressure-equipment manufacturer; 332410 is more descriptive.
Verification Notes / Uncertainty: Product scope is first-party; the ownership note reflects a pending, not completed, transaction.

What They Make or Do: Babcock Marine’s Rosyth operation manufactures and assembles submarine missile-tube structures, particularly tactical missile-tube assemblies for the U.S.–U.K. Common Missile Compartment used by Columbia- and Dreadnought-class submarines.
Primary Products or Capabilities: Common Missile Compartment tactical missile-tube assemblies; large precision-fabricated submarine structures; welding, machining, assembly, inspection and test.
Verified Ultimate Owner: Babcock International Group plc (United Kingdom; public company)
Operating Entity / Site Note: This is the Babcock Marine (Rosyth) legal operating entity in Scotland. It should be distinguished from the separate Rosyth Royal Dockyard Limited UEI at rank 34, although both operate within Babcock’s Rosyth enterprise.
NAICS Assessment: Missing. No primary NAICS is resolved in the file. The demonstrated activity is heavy naval/submarine module manufacturing and integration.
Verification Notes / Uncertainty: Babcock directly identifies Rosyth work on missile tubes for Electric Boat and the Common Missile Compartment.

Vendor (registry name): GLOBE COMPOSITE SOLUTIONS, LTD., LIMITED PARTNERSHIP
What They Make or Do: Globe Composite Solutions engineers and manufactures molded composite, elastomeric and syntactic-material components for submarines and other undersea platforms, including acoustic and hydrodynamic structures.
Primary Products or Capabilities: Submarine sonar acoustic panels and baffles; hydrophone and sonar casings; fairings; propulsor covers and nosecones; specialty elastomer parts, gaskets, coatings and syntactic materials.
Operating Entity / Site Note: The legal name in the file is a limited partnership; Globe operates as an ESCO Technologies naval-composites business.
NAICS Assessment: Misleading as primary. NAICS 325212 implies synthetic-rubber manufacturing, while Globe’s durable role is engineering and molding finished composite/elastomer components and acoustic structures.
Verification Notes / Uncertainty: Globe’s first-party capability pages directly describe its undersea products; ESCO ownership is reflected in current corporate materials.

What They Make or Do: Precision Custom Components manufactures large custom pressure vessels, heat exchangers, heavy-walled weldments and precision-machined nuclear and naval equipment.
Primary Products or Capabilities: Pressure vessels and reactor vessels; large heat exchangers; heavy plate weldments; large-envelope CNC machining; welding, NDE, hydrostatic testing and final assembly.
Verified Ultimate Owner: Privately held Precision Components Group; BWXT acquisition announced but not closed as of June 18, 2026
Operating Entity / Site Note: The York, Pennsylvania company is a specialized heavy-manufacturing operating entity. BWXT’s April 2026 agreement to buy Precision Components Group was expected to close in the second half of 2026 and is not treated as completed.
NAICS Assessment: Partially correct. NAICS 332313 captures plate-work manufacturing, but not the full pressure-vessel, heat-exchanger, large machining and nuclear-quality scope.
Verification Notes / Uncertainty: Capabilities and parent are well supported; individual Navy end items are not publicly itemized, so the profile stays at the product-family level.

What They Make or Do: York International’s Navy-focused operation supplies YORK shipboard air-conditioning and refrigeration plants, compressors, chillers and condensers for DDG-51 destroyers and other naval vessels.
Primary Products or Capabilities: Shipboard air-conditioning plants; chillers; compressors; condensers; refrigeration machinery; controls, spares and lifecycle support.
Verified Corporate Parent: Johnson Controls, Inc. / Johnson Controls Navy Systems
Verified Ultimate Owner: Johnson Controls International plc (Ireland; public company)
Operating Entity / Site Note: The UEI is the York International legal manufacturer within Johnson Controls. The relevant activity is Navy-qualified YORK machinery, not the broader commercial HVAC enterprise.
NAICS Assessment: Missing. The file has no resolved primary NAICS. NAICS 333415 would closely describe the demonstrated HVAC and refrigeration-equipment activity.
Verification Notes / Uncertainty: Johnson Controls directly identifies YORK Navy HVAC products; a SAM notice corroborates the DDG-51 air-conditioning-plant context.

Primary NAICS Description: Speed Changer, Industrial High-Speed Drive, and Gear Manufacturing
What They Make or Do: Timken Gears & Services, through Philadelphia Gear, designs and manufactures main reduction gears and other large marine gearboxes for Navy surface combatants, including DDG-51.
Primary Products or Capabilities: DDG-51 main reduction gear sets; naval reduction gearboxes; high-speed gears and pinions; testing, overhaul, repair and lifecycle engineering.
Operating Entity / Site Note: The legal entity is Timken Gears & Services; Philadelphia Gear is the operating brand and heritage business responsible for the naval gear products.
NAICS Assessment: Correct. NAICS 333612 accurately describes speed-changer and gear manufacturing and is strongly representative of the operating entity.
Verification Notes / Uncertainty: Timken directly documents both the product and DDG-51 program role.

What They Make or Do: SOCAIL, LDA appears to be a Portuguese industrial-piping contractor whose name expands to Sociedade de Canalizações Industriais. Public directories describe industrial pipework and water/gas piping installation; a specific DDG-51 deliverable could not be verified.
Primary Products or Capabilities: Industrial piping and pipework installation; piping fabrication/assembly; water and gas piping services. Exact naval product or work package is unverified.
Operating Entity / Site Note: The operating entity is reported in Seixal, Portugal. No authoritative company website, current ownership filing or public Navy contract description was located.
NAICS Assessment: Missing. No primary NAICS is resolved in the file. Available evidence points to industrial piping/installation, but the exact manufacturing-versus-installation mix is uncertain.
Verification Notes / Uncertainty: Public evidence is limited to business directories. The dataset shows DDG-51 subaward dollars, but the specific shipyard, equipment, system or onboard deliverable cannot be responsibly inferred.

What They Make or Do: Rhoads Metal Fabrications, operating within Rhoads Industries at the Philadelphia Navy Yard, fabricates, machines, outfits and integrates large submarine structures and machinery modules.
Primary Products or Capabilities: Large submarine structural modules; decks and platforms; pressure vessels; turbine-generator skids; heavy plate fabrications; complex welding, machining, outfitting and pier-side integration.
Verified Ultimate Owner: Family-controlled trust ownership; SAM file reports The RMF Irrevocable Deed of Trust
Operating Entity / Site Note: The UEI is the metal-fabrication operating company. Rhoads Industries is the public operating brand. The trust shown in SAM is retained as an ownership note because a current first-party ownership filing was not located.
NAICS Assessment: Missing. No resolved primary NAICS is shown. The demonstrated activity is heavy structural-metal fabrication, machining and module integration for Navy shipbuilding.
Verification Notes / Uncertainty: High confidence in operating activity and Navy role; medium confidence in the precise current trust/ultimate-owner description.

What They Make or Do: Portland Valve manufactures Navy-qualified ball valves and other specialty ship-service valves, and provides repair and refurbishment support for submarines and surface ships.
Primary Products or Capabilities: Ship-service ball valves; specialty naval valves; valve actuators and assemblies; replacement parts; overhaul, repair and testing.
Operating Entity / Site Note: Portland Valve is a CIRCOR naval brand and operating business. A May 2026 agreement to sell CIRCOR’s commercial and defense aerospace business to Parker Hannifin did not include the retained naval business.
NAICS Assessment: Correct. NAICS 332911 accurately reflects industrial-valve manufacturing.
Verification Notes / Uncertainty: Current brand, product and ownership status are supported by CIRCOR and KKR; the 2026 aerospace-only transaction is noted to avoid a false parent change.

What They Make or Do: Curtiss-Wright’s Target Rock operation in East Farmingdale, New York designs and manufactures critical-service valves and actuators for naval nuclear propulsion and other ship systems.
Primary Products or Capabilities: Nuclear safety and relief valves; control, check and isolation valves; electrohydraulic and electromechanical actuators; complete valve-actuator assemblies; qualification testing and support.
Verified Corporate Parent: Curtiss-Wright Flow Control Corporation / Curtiss-Wright Electro-Mechanical Systems
Operating Entity / Site Note: CAGE 95169 corresponds to the Target Rock operating unit, not Curtiss-Wright’s other pump, motor or generator divisions.
NAICS Assessment: Correct. NAICS 332911 accurately describes the core industrial-valve manufacturing activity.
Verification Notes / Uncertainty: Target Rock first-party pages directly document its naval nuclear valve and actuator products.

What They Make or Do: Curtiss-Wright’s Electro-Mechanical Division in Cheswick, Pennsylvania designs and manufactures large electric machines and turbomachinery for U.S. naval nuclear propulsion and ship-service power systems.
Primary Products or Capabilities: Large propulsion motors and generators; ship-service turbine generators; secondary propulsion systems; specialized pumps and turbomachinery; engineering, manufacturing, qualification and lifecycle support.
Operating Entity / Site Note: CAGE 04808 identifies the Cheswick Electro-Mechanical Division. It is a different operating site and product family from the Bethlehem Engineered Pump Division at rank 4.
NAICS Assessment: Partially correct. NAICS 335312 captures motors and generators, but not the division’s full naval turbomachinery, secondary-propulsion and nuclear-qualified-system scope.
Verification Notes / Uncertainty: Operating site and product scope are directly documented by Curtiss-Wright.

Primary NAICS Description: Search, Detection, Navigation, Guidance, Aeronautical, and Nautical System and Instrument Manufacturing
What They Make or Do: Northrop Grumman’s Charlottesville maritime operation designs and produces integrated bridge, navigation, steering and ship-control systems for U.S. Navy surface ships, including DDG-51.
Primary Products or Capabilities: Integrated bridge and navigation systems; inertial navigation sensors; steering-gear systems; ship-control consoles, displays and associated software/hardware integration.
Operating Entity / Site Note: CAGE 03956 is associated with the Charlottesville, Virginia maritime-navigation and ship-controls operation, distinct from Northrop Grumman’s Sunnyvale naval-power row.
NAICS Assessment: Correct. NAICS 334511 is a good match for navigation, nautical, guidance and control-instrument manufacturing, although it does not fully convey system integration.
Verification Notes / Uncertainty: Northrop Grumman releases identify the exact maritime product lines and DDG-51 application.

Primary NAICS Description: Fluid Power Valve and Hose Fitting Manufacturing
What They Make or Do: VACCO Industries designs and manufactures precision, low-noise fluid-control components and subsystems for submarines, surface ships and other demanding defense applications.
Primary Products or Capabilities: Quiet naval valves; manifolds and integrated fluid-control assemblies; regulators; filters; check, relief and isolation valves; precision welding and test.
Operating Entity / Site Note: VACCO was previously owned by ESCO Technologies. RBC Bearings completed its acquisition on July 21, 2025, so older SAM or web records naming ESCO are stale.
NAICS Assessment: Correct. NAICS 332912 is well aligned with precision fluid-power valves and fittings, though it does not show VACCO’s ultraquiet naval specialization.
Verification Notes / Uncertainty: Products are first-party and the 2025 ownership change is documented by the acquirer.

What They Make or Do: L-3 Communications Westwood, now operating within L3Harris Maritime Power & Energy Solutions, engineers and manufactures military shipboard electrical power-distribution equipment.
Primary Products or Capabilities: Military switchboards and load centers; power-distribution panels; control consoles and workstations; panelboards; circuit-protection and monitoring assemblies.
Operating Entity / Site Note: The file retains the legacy L-3 Communications Westwood legal name. Current product pages place the business in L3Harris’ maritime power portfolio.
NAICS Assessment: Correct. NAICS 335313 accurately describes switchgear and switchboard apparatus manufacturing.
Verification Notes / Uncertainty: L3Harris first-party product pages are specific and current.

What They Make or Do: Huntington Ingalls’ Newport News Shipbuilding operation builds and integrates major nuclear-submarine modules and sections and is a principal construction partner on Virginia- and Columbia-class submarines.
Primary Products or Capabilities: Virginia-class submarine modules and sections; Columbia-class sterns and major module sections; nuclear-ship construction, structural fabrication, outfitting, integration, test and delivery.
Operating Entity / Site Note: CAGE 43689 is associated with Newport News Shipbuilding. The legal row name is Huntington Ingalls Incorporated; the operating shipyard identity is the critical fact.
NAICS Assessment: Correct. NAICS 336611 accurately describes the operating entity’s whole-ship and major-module shipbuilding activity.
Verification Notes / Uncertainty: HII directly documents its submarine construction roles and delivered modules.

Primary NAICS Description: Rubber Product Manufacturing for Mechanical Use
What They Make or Do: Oil States Industries’ Special Products operation manufactures Navy-qualified elastomeric shock- and vibration-isolation hardware used to mount and support shipboard machinery and piping.
Primary Products or Capabilities: Resilient equipment mounts; elastomeric shock and vibration isolators; pipe hangers and clamps; bonded rubber-to-metal isolation components; qualification and replacement hardware.
Operating Entity / Site Note: CAGE 18444 is associated with the Special Products operation. The public corporate site gives limited detail on individual Navy part families; federal item records supply product-level corroboration.
NAICS Assessment: Partially correct. NAICS 326291 broadly fits molded rubber products for mechanical use, but does not identify the specialized Navy shock/vibration-isolation function.
Verification Notes / Uncertainty: Corporate ownership and elastomer capability are clear. The exact Navy products rely partly on federal procurement/NSN records rather than a detailed current first-party catalog.

What They Make or Do: APCO Technologies SA designs, manufactures and commissions custom automated machinery and production systems, including precision positioning, robotic handling and welding equipment used to industrialize complex ship-structure manufacturing.
Primary Products or Capabilities: Custom positioning systems; robotic and automated handling equipment; welding and assembly systems; special-purpose production machinery; engineering, integration, factory acceptance testing and commissioning.
Operating Entity / Site Note: The operating entity is a Swiss engineering and manufacturing company. The most plausible shipbuilding role is delivery of shipyard production equipment or automation rather than equipment installed aboard a ship.
NAICS Assessment: Missing. No primary NAICS is resolved. The demonstrated activity is engineered industrial machinery, robotics and systems integration.
Verification Notes / Uncertainty: High confidence in products and ownership; only partial for onboard ship supply because public evidence supports ship-production equipment rather than a specific installed naval end item.

Primary NAICS Description: Capacitor, Resistor, Coil, Transformer, and Other Inductor Manufacturing
What They Make or Do: EMS Development Corporation designs and manufactures naval magnetic-signature-management and power-management systems, including degaussing equipment, power electronics and magnetic components.
Primary Products or Capabilities: Degaussing and magnetic-field countermeasure systems; power converters and amplifiers; transformers, inductors and magnetic components; controls and monitoring electronics for surface ships and submarines.
Verified Corporate Parent: ESCO Maritime Solutions / ESCO Technologies Inc.
Operating Entity / Site Note: EMS was acquired from Ultra Maritime as part of ESCO’s Signature Management & Power business on April 25, 2025. Older records naming Ultra Electronics are stale.
NAICS Assessment: Partially correct. NAICS 334416 captures transformers, inductors and related magnetics, but not the integrated degaussing, signature-management and power-electronics systems.
Verification Notes / Uncertainty: Current parent is established by ESCO’s completed-acquisition release; products are corroborated by operating-entity and federal registration sources.

What They Make or Do: Teledyne Instruments’ Teledyne D.G. O’Brien operation manufactures submarine pressure-hull electrical, fiber-optic and radio-frequency penetrators, underwater connectors and molded cable assemblies.
Primary Products or Capabilities: Pressure-hull penetrators; electrical, fiber-optic and RF penetrators; watertight and underwater connectors; molded cable assemblies; hull glands and feedthroughs.
Operating Entity / Site Note: CAGE 17476 is associated with Teledyne D.G. O’Brien, a specialized Teledyne Marine operation; it should not be profiled as the whole diversified Teledyne Instruments business.
NAICS Assessment: Correct. NAICS 334417 closely matches electronic connector manufacturing, though pressure-hull penetration assemblies are more specialized than the code suggests.
Verification Notes / Uncertainty: Teledyne first-party product pages precisely identify the submarine components.

What They Make or Do: Graham Corporation designs and manufactures custom heat-transfer, vacuum and fluid-management equipment for naval nuclear propulsion and power systems.
Primary Products or Capabilities: Surface condensers and heat exchangers; steam-jet ejectors and vacuum systems; thermal/fluid-management skids; custom pumps and process equipment; engineering, fabrication and testing.
Operating Entity / Site Note: The UEI represents the Batavia, New York operating company. Graham is a first-tier equipment maker, not a shipyard.
NAICS Assessment: Misleading. NAICS 336611 labels shipbuilding, but Graham supplies specialized thermal and fluid equipment; heat-exchanger and process-equipment manufacturing codes would be more descriptive.
Verification Notes / Uncertainty: SEC filings and company releases directly describe naval nuclear products and deliveries.

What They Make or Do: ELLWOOD National Forge produces large open-die forgings and finish-machined rotating components for defense shipbuilding, supplying shipyards, propulsion-system integrators and OEMs.
Primary Products or Capabilities: Long high-ratio forged shafts and sleeves; rings and discs; high-speed rotating components; heat treatment, rough and finish machining, inspection and testing.
Operating Entity / Site Note: The UEI is the Irvine, Pennsylvania forging company within the ELLWOOD group. It supplies forged or finish-machined parts rather than integrated propulsion machinery.
NAICS Assessment: Correct. NAICS 332111 accurately describes the primary forging process; the company’s finish machining and defense-shipbuilding specialization add important context.
Verification Notes / Uncertainty: ELLWOOD’s official shipbuilding page explicitly describes mission-critical defense ship components and finish-machined rotating parts.

What They Make or Do: Austal USA fabricates, outfits and integrates modular command, control and payload structures for U.S. nuclear submarines in addition to its broader shipbuilding work.
Primary Products or Capabilities: Virginia-class Command and Control Systems Modules; Electronic Deck Modules; Columbia-class Outboard Payload Deck modules; structural fabrication, outfitting, assembly and test.
Operating Entity / Site Note: The Mobile, Alabama operating company is a U.S. shipbuilder and module supplier within Australian parent Austal Limited.
NAICS Assessment: Correct. NAICS 336611 accurately reflects shipbuilding and major-module construction.
Verification Notes / Uncertainty: Austal USA’s official submarine page identifies the specific modules and programs.

Primary NAICS Description: Military Armored Vehicle, Tank, and Tank Component Manufacturing
What They Make or Do: W International, LLC was the legacy corporate/operating entity for large-scale naval metal fabrication and module assembly associated with the Goose Creek, South Carolina complex. Huntington Ingalls acquired substantially all operating assets of related W International SC and Vivid Empire businesses in January 2025.
Primary Products or Capabilities: Complex steel structures and ship modules; heavy plate cutting, forming, machining and welding; large assembly and outfitting for naval shipbuilding.
Verified Corporate Parent: Legacy W International, LLC; operating assets largely transitioned to HII/Newport News Shipbuilding–Charleston Operations
Verified Ultimate Owner: Legacy private ownership not verified; acquired operating assets are owned by Huntington Ingalls Industries, Inc.
Operating Entity / Site Note: This UEI is not automatically treated as an HII subsidiary. The available SEC disclosure describes an asset acquisition, not acquisition of every legacy legal entity; later legal reporting still names W International, LLC separately.
NAICS Assessment: Misleading. NAICS 336992 concerns military armored vehicles and tank components, not naval structural fabrication or ship modules.
Verification Notes / Uncertainty: High confidence in historical operating activity and asset transfer; lower confidence in the continuing legal status, ownership and present contracting use of this exact UEI.

What They Make or Do: Hunt Valve manufactures severe-duty Navy valves and electromechanical valve actuators for submarine and surface-ship fluid systems.
Primary Products or Capabilities: Ball, gate, globe and check valves; high-pressure and severe-duty naval valves; electromechanical actuators; valve-actuator assemblies; repair and lifecycle support.
Operating Entity / Site Note: Hunt Valve is an operating company/brand within Fairbanks Morse Defense. FMD acquired Hunt Valve in 2021.
NAICS Assessment: Correct. NAICS 332911 accurately describes industrial-valve manufacturing.
Verification Notes / Uncertainty: Fairbanks Morse Defense directly documents ownership, products and Virginia-/Columbia-class orders.

What They Make or Do: Rosyth Royal Dockyard Limited operates within Babcock’s Rosyth shipyard enterprise, performing heavy naval fabrication, assembly and integration, including Common Missile Compartment tactical missile-tube assemblies.
Primary Products or Capabilities: Submarine missile-tube assemblies; large complex naval structures; shipyard fabrication, welding, machining, assembly, integration, inspection and test.
Verified Ultimate Owner: Babcock International Group plc (United Kingdom; public company)
Operating Entity / Site Note: This is a separate legal UEI from Babcock Marine (Rosyth) Limited at rank 11, but both are part of the same Rosyth operating complex. Public product reporting is generally at Babcock/Rosyth group level.
NAICS Assessment: Correct. NAICS 336611 broadly matches the shipyard and major naval-module manufacturing activity.
Verification Notes / Uncertainty: Ownership is explicit in the file and federal entity data; Babcock’s group-level contract release supports the missile-tube work.

What They Make or Do: SPD Electrical Systems, now operated as L3Harris Maritime Power & Energy Solutions, manufactures Navy-qualified shipboard circuit breakers and associated power-distribution hardware.
Primary Products or Capabilities: Low- and medium-voltage shipboard circuit breakers; breaker assemblies and replacement/racking parts; switchgear components; protection and power-distribution hardware.
Verified Corporate Parent: L3Harris Maritime Power & Energy Solutions, Inc.
Operating Entity / Site Note: The file uses the legacy SPD Electrical Systems name. Current federal records identify L3Harris Maritime Power & Energy Solutions, Inc. doing business as SPD under the same CAGE context.
NAICS Assessment: Correct. NAICS 335313 is a good match for switchgear and switchboard-apparatus manufacturing.
Verification Notes / Uncertainty: Current legal/DBA identity and OEM circuit-breaker products are corroborated by federal records.

What They Make or Do: W International SC, LLC operated the Goose Creek, South Carolina advanced-fabrication facility that produced large naval ship structures and modules. Huntington Ingalls acquired substantially all of its operating assets in January 2025, and the site now operates as Newport News Shipbuilding–Charleston Operations.
Primary Products or Capabilities: Large shipbuilding structures and modules; heavy plate fabrication; cutting, forming, machining, welding, assembly and outfitting.
Verified Corporate Parent: Legacy parent W International, LLC; operating assets now within HII/Newport News Shipbuilding–Charleston Operations
Verified Ultimate Owner: Legacy legal ownership not fully verified; acquired operating assets are owned by Huntington Ingalls Industries, Inc.
Operating Entity / Site Note: The transaction was an asset acquisition. The legacy UEI may not represent the current HII operating registration, so historical subaward attribution and present outreach identity should be kept separate.
NAICS Assessment: Correct for historical operations. NAICS 336611 fits the facility’s naval module and ship-structure manufacturing, though the entity was not a complete-ship prime.
Verification Notes / Uncertainty: High confidence in historical work and current facility operator; medium confidence in the continuing legal status and ownership of the exact legacy LLC/UEI.

Primary NAICS Description: Air-Conditioning and Warm Air Heating Equipment and Commercial and Industrial Refrigeration Equipment Manufacturing
What They Make or Do: Engineered Coil Company, operating as Leonardo DRS Marlo Coil/Marlo Heat Transfer Solutions in High Ridge, Missouri, manufactures Navy-qualified heat-transfer and HVAC components.
Primary Products or Capabilities: Heating and cooling coils; fan-coil units and assemblies; condensers and evaporators; refrigeration and air-handling heat exchangers; custom Navy and marine coils.
Operating Entity / Site Note: The legal entity is Engineered Coil Company; the public operating brand is Marlo Coil/Marlo Heat Transfer Solutions within Leonardo DRS Naval Power Systems.
NAICS Assessment: Correct. NAICS 333415 accurately describes the HVAC and refrigeration-equipment manufacturing activity.
Verification Notes / Uncertainty: Leonardo DRS first-party pages identify the exact site and Navy coil products.

Primary NAICS Description: Fluid Power Valve and Hose Fitting Manufacturing
What They Make or Do: Marotta Controls designs and manufactures high-reliability shipboard valves, manifolds and flow-control systems for submarine and surface-ship pneumatic and fluid systems.
Primary Products or Capabilities: High- and low-pressure valves; manifold assemblies; quieting manifold technology; pneumatic and hydraulic flow controls; custom shipboard controls and qualification testing.
Operating Entity / Site Note: The UEI represents the Montville, New Jersey operating company; no larger corporate parent was verified.
NAICS Assessment: Correct. NAICS 332912 is a good match for fluid-power valves and fittings, though Marotta also integrates complete manifolds and controls.
Verification Notes / Uncertainty: Marotta first-party pages directly document naval markets and shipboard flow-control products.

Primary NAICS Description: All Other Miscellaneous Electrical Equipment and Component Manufacturing
What They Make or Do: Espey designs and manufactures military power electronics and magnetic components for naval platforms, including transformers, power converters, filters, battery chargers and radar/transmitter power equipment.
Primary Products or Capabilities: Power-distribution transformers; power supplies and converters; filters and inductors; battery chargers; high-voltage/radar power equipment; custom magnetics and power assemblies.
Operating Entity / Site Note: The Saratoga Springs operating company is independent rather than a subsidiary of a diversified OEM.
NAICS Assessment: Partially correct. NAICS 335999 broadly covers other electrical equipment, but is nonspecific; transformer, power-conversion and electronic-equipment codes better communicate the actual product families.
Verification Notes / Uncertainty: Espey’s official military-products page details the naval power and magnetic products.

Primary NAICS Description: Laminated Plastics Plate, Sheet (except Packaging), and Shape Manufacturing
What They Make or Do: Goodrich’s Jacksonville Engineered Polymer Products operation, now part of Collins Aerospace, manufactures molded and vulcanized elastomer and composite acoustic components for submarines and surface ships.
Primary Products or Capabilities: Sonar windows, baffles and reflectors; bow-dome boots; acoustic tiles; rubber sonar domes; molded urethane/rubber and composite hydrodynamic or propulsor-related components.
Operating Entity / Site Note: CAGE 0BBK9 is associated with the Jacksonville Engineered Polymer Products operation. The file’s highest owner, Raytheon Technologies Corporation, is a stale corporate name; the parent renamed itself RTX Corporation in July 2023.
NAICS Assessment: Misleading. NAICS 326130 concerns laminated-plastics sheet and shapes, while this operation makes engineered finished elastomer/composite acoustic products; molded rubber and plastics-product codes are more representative.
Verification Notes / Uncertainty: Collins Aerospace directly documents the submarine and surface-ship composite/rubber products; current ultimate-owner name is verified by RTX.

What They Make or Do: Advance Manufacturing Company is a precision machine shop and fabricator producing complex, close-tolerance machined parts, weldments and assemblies for defense, nuclear and other high-specification customers.
Primary Products or Capabilities: Multi-axis CNC-machined parts; large and close-tolerance components; welded and machined assemblies; EDM; inspection, NDE and quality-controlled build-to-print manufacturing.
Operating Entity / Site Note: The UEI represents the Westfield, Massachusetts operating company. Public sources confirm defense and nuclear work but do not identify the exact submarine component represented by these subawards.
NAICS Assessment: Correct. NAICS 332710 accurately describes the machine-shop activity, though it does not convey defense/nuclear quality requirements or assembly capability.
Verification Notes / Uncertainty: High confidence in operating capability and ownership; medium overall because the exact Navy shipbuilding end items are not publicly disclosed.

Primary NAICS Description: Fluid Power Cylinder and Actuator Manufacturing
What They Make or Do: Moog’s naval systems operation designs and manufactures low-noise hydraulic and electromechanical motion-control actuators for mission-critical submarine functions.
Primary Products or Capabilities: Ballast-water control actuators; torpedo-launch and weapon-system actuators; emergency-surfacing actuators; hydraulic and electromechanical linear/rotary actuators; control electronics and qualification testing.
Operating Entity / Site Note: CAGE 07270 is associated with Moog’s Orrville, Ohio operation. The profile focuses on the naval actuator products tied to that operating location rather than Moog’s aerospace portfolio.
NAICS Assessment: Correct. NAICS 333995 accurately describes fluid-power cylinder and actuator manufacturing.
Verification Notes / Uncertainty: Moog directly identifies Virginia-class mission-critical actuator applications.

Primary NAICS Description: Measuring, Dispensing, and Other Pumping Equipment Manufacturing
What They Make or Do: Warren Pumps, now operated as CIRCOR Naval Solutions, designs and manufactures highly engineered pumps for U.S. Navy submarines and surface ships.
Primary Products or Capabilities: Centrifugal pumps; gear, piston and twin-screw pumps; Navy-qualified pump assemblies; replacement parts; overhaul, repair, test and lifecycle engineering.
Verified Corporate Parent: CIRCOR Naval Solutions, LLC / CIRCOR International
Operating Entity / Site Note: The file uses Warren Pumps LLC. Current federal records identify CIRCOR Naval Solutions LLC doing business as Warren Pumps. A May 2026 proposed CIRCOR aerospace sale did not include the retained naval business.
NAICS Assessment: Correct. NAICS 333914 is a strong match for pump manufacturing.
Verification Notes / Uncertainty: Warren and CIRCOR first-party pages document the naval pump portfolio; current ownership is retained with CIRCOR/KKR.

Primary NAICS Description: Instrument Manufacturing for Measuring and Testing Electricity and Electrical Signals
What They Make or Do: DRS Consolidated Controls, now part of Leonardo DRS Nuclear Instrumentation & Control, designs and manufactures safety-critical instrumentation, control and actuation systems for naval nuclear propulsion plants.
Primary Products or Capabilities: Analog and digital nuclear instrumentation and control systems; safety and control cabinets; sensors and transmitters; electric-actuation conversion systems; qualification, manufacturing, test and lifecycle support.
Operating Entity / Site Note: The legal row is DRS Consolidated Controls in Danbury, Connecticut; current public branding is Leonardo DRS Nuclear Instrumentation & Control.
NAICS Assessment: Partially correct. NAICS 334515 covers electrical measuring and testing instruments but understates the complete safety-critical control-system, cabinet and actuation-integration role.
Verification Notes / Uncertainty: Leonardo DRS directly documents the exact site, products and naval nuclear application.

What They Make or Do: CP Industries manufactures very large seamless high-pressure steel cylinders and storage vessels for compressed gases, including military and naval shipboard gas-storage applications.
Primary Products or Capabilities: Large seamless high-pressure cylinders; compressed-air and industrial-gas storage vessels; jumbo tubes and multi-cylinder skids; naval oxygen, nitrogen and air-storage containers.
Verified Ultimate Owner: Everest Kanto Cylinder Limited (India; public company)
Operating Entity / Site Note: The McKeesport, Pennsylvania manufacturer is a U.S. operating subsidiary of India-based Everest Kanto Cylinder.
NAICS Assessment: Correct. NAICS 332420 accurately describes heavy-gauge metal tank and vessel manufacturing.
Verification Notes / Uncertainty: CP Industries and its parent directly document the product family, military applications and ownership.

What They Make or Do: PCB Piezotronics designs and manufactures piezoelectric sensing equipment used for underwater acoustics and shipboard shock, vibration, pressure and machinery monitoring.
Primary Products or Capabilities: Underwater pressure sensors and hydrophones; accelerometers; shock and vibration sensors; dynamic pressure sensors; signal conditioners; custom OEM sensing assemblies.
Operating Entity / Site Note: PCB is a wholly owned Amphenol company. The UEI is the Depew, New York sensor manufacturer rather than the whole Amphenol group.
NAICS Assessment: Partially correct. NAICS 334419 broadly covers other electronic components, but sensor/instrument codes such as 334519 better communicate the operating company’s main products.
Verification Notes / Uncertainty: PCB first-party pages establish the underwater/OEM products and Amphenol ownership; an NSN record corroborates a Virginia-class application.

What They Make or Do: Pegasus Steel, now part of the Keel defense-manufacturing platform, fabricates and integrates complex, fracture-critical steel structures and outfitted modules for submarines, aircraft carriers and other naval platforms.
Primary Products or Capabilities: Complex welded steel structures; outfitted submarine modules; habitation and equipment modules; heavy plate cutting, forming, machining and certified welding; large assembly and test.
Verified Ultimate Owner: Arlington Capital Partners VI, L.P. and affiliated funds
Operating Entity / Site Note: Pegasus Steel was acquired by Arlington Capital Partners and is now operated within the Keel platform. The UEI remains the Pegasus operating entity.
NAICS Assessment: Correct. NAICS 332313 accurately describes plate-work manufacturing, though it understates the outfitted-module and integration content.
Verification Notes / Uncertainty: Keel and Arlington directly document the acquisition, shipbuilding platform and Virginia-class case study.

Vendor (registry name): KME AMERICA MARINE TUBE & FITTING, LLC
Primary NAICS Description: Other Metal Valve and Pipe Fitting Manufacturing
What They Make or Do: KME America Marine Tube & Fitting manufactures complex metallic formed fittings and flanges for U.S. Navy aircraft carriers and submarines, using heavy forming, CNC machining and advanced metalworking processes.
Primary Products or Capabilities: Marine pipe and tube fittings; elbows, tees, reducers and formed branches; flanges; copper-alloy and other corrosion-resistant metallic piping components; pressing, machining and inspection.
Verified Corporate Parent: KME America Marine Holding, Inc. / cunova operating group
Verified Ultimate Owner: cunova GmbH (Germany; current highest owner reported in SAM)
Operating Entity / Site Note: The Jacksonville, Florida company is a U.S. manufacturing operation listed on cunova’s current contact page. A 2024 corporate transaction involving cunova and KME Aerospace creates a complex ownership history; the current SAM highest-owner field and cunova listing are used rather than inferring beyond them.
NAICS Assessment: Correct. NAICS 332919 closely matches metal valve and pipe-fitting manufacturing and is representative of the company’s output.
Verification Notes / Uncertainty: First-party KME and cunova pages establish the product, Navy application and current group relationship; ownership structure is recorded conservatively.

What They Make or Do: RIX Industries designs and manufactures oil-free high-pressure gas compressors and integrated gas-generation systems for naval ships and submarines.
Primary Products or Capabilities: Oil-free reciprocating air and gas compressors; oxygen- and special-gas compressors; shipboard nitrogen-generation systems; compressor plants, controls, skids, spares and lifecycle support.
Operating Entity / Site Note: The UEI represents the Benicia, California operating company. SAM reports Crestt, LP as the immediate owner; no higher public parent was verified.
NAICS Assessment: Correct. NAICS 333912 accurately describes air and gas compressor manufacturing, although RIX also integrates complete gas-generation systems.
Verification Notes / Uncertainty: RIX first-party pages directly describe its naval compressor and nitrogen-generation products.

What They Make or Do: BWX Technologies, Inc. is the publicly traded corporate parent and managing office for BWXT’s businesses. Its operating subsidiaries—especially BWXT Nuclear Operations Group—manufacture naval nuclear-reactor components, fuel and propulsion-plant assemblies; the exact deliverable booked to this corporate UEI is not publicly separable.
Primary Products or Capabilities: Corporate management and award/reporting functions; group-level naval nuclear propulsion engineering and manufacturing performed through operating subsidiaries.
Verified Corporate Parent: None; this entity is the corporate parent
Verified Ultimate Owner: BWX Technologies, Inc. (public company; owned by public shareholders)
Operating Entity / Site Note: This UEI appears to be the corporate managing-office registration rather than a production plant. It should not be merged with the rank-9 BWXT Nuclear Operations Group UEI when describing operating output.
NAICS Assessment: File field is missing, but current federal-registration data identify NAICS 551114 (Corporate, Subsidiary, and Regional Managing Offices), which is appropriate for this exact corporate UEI. It would be misleading if interpreted as the NAICS for BWXT’s manufacturing subsidiaries.
Verification Notes / Uncertainty: High confidence that this is the corporate parent/office and that BWXT subsidiaries supply naval nuclear hardware; uncertainty remains about why subaward dollars were attributed to this UEI and what, if anything, the corporate entity itself delivered.

Primary NAICS Description: Commercial and Service Industry Machinery Manufacturing
What They Make or Do: Parker's Carson, CA Water Purification / Racor-Village Marine Tec operation: designs, builds, tests, supports MIL-SPEC reverse-osmosis desalination/watermaker systems (potable & reactor-grade water) for Navy surface ships, submarines, carriers; plus spares, technical assistance, repair.
Primary Products or Capabilities: MIL-SPEC reverse-osmosis desalination/watermaker systems; potable & reactor-grade water systems; spares; system repair.
Verified Ultimate Owner: Parker-Hannifin Corporation, NYSE: PH (public; no higher parent)
Operating Entity / Site Note: UEI is the Carson, CA Water Purification / Racor-Village Marine Tec establishment, not a generic Parker machinery site.
NAICS Assessment: MISLEADING - 333310 'Commercial & Service Industry Machinery' is too generic; obscures the naval desalination-system OEM role; not HVAC/service machinery.
Verification Notes / Uncertainty: Operating site & naval desalination role established by Parker MIL-SPEC RO literature, SAM, USAspending; subaward-line content not inferred.

Primary NAICS Description: Power Boiler and Heat Exchanger Manufacturing
What They Make or Do: U.S. Alfa Laval entity: heat exchangers, centrifugal separators/decanters, filters/strainers/membranes, pumps, valves, marine pumping systems + parts/service. Naval outputs = heat-transfer, liquid-separation, fluid-handling.
Primary Products or Capabilities: Heat exchangers; centrifugal separators/decanters; filtration; pumps; valves; marine pumping systems.
Verified Corporate Parent: Alfa Laval AB (publ), Lund, Sweden (100% owner)
Verified Ultimate Owner: Alfa Laval AB public shareholders, Nasdaq Stockholm (Winder Holding AG 29.53%, not majority)
Operating Entity / Site Note: U.S. corporation under Swedish ultimate parent.
NAICS Assessment: ACCURATE but INCOMPLETE - 332410 describes the heat-exchanger line (not misleading) but omits separators, filtration, pumps, valves, marine pumping.
Verification Notes / Uncertainty: Products/ownership from Alfa Laval pages + 2025 annual report; subaward content not inferred.

What They Make or Do: Mississauga operation, now Trident Maritime Systems Canada: bespoke marine fuel- & lube-oil filtration/separation systems (diesel pre-filter coalescers, AVCAT filter-separators), specialized valves/actuators, aviation-support equipment, component overhaul; filtration installed broadly across USN fleet.
Primary Products or Capabilities: Marine fuel/lube-oil filtration & separation systems; filter-separators; specialized valves/actuators.
Verified Corporate Parent: Lake Shore Systems, Inc. (AAE transferred 2025-03-01); group: Trident Maritime Systems
Verified Ultimate Owner: Funds managed by J.F. Lehman & Company (Trident a current JFLCO portfolio company)
Operating Entity / Site Note: Canadian legal entity & manufacturing operation; U.S. PE-controlled group; trades as Trident Maritime Systems Canada.
NAICS Assessment: MISLEADING - 332420 'Metal Tank Mfg' understates engineered naval filtration/separation systems (proprietary internals, qualification, testing, integration).
Verification Notes / Uncertainty: Identity/ownership from SAM, Trident materials, Companies House accounts, JFLCO portfolio; ownership transfer dated 2025-03-01.

What They Make or Do: Designs/builds pressure-vessel-based fluid-energy & hydraulic-transient control equipment: bladder surge tanks, surge arrestors, pulsation dampeners, related pressure vessels. ASME Sec VIII, NAVSEA Level I/SUBSAFE; used aboard nuclear submarines.
Primary Products or Capabilities: Bladder surge tanks; surge arrestors; pulsation dampeners; ASME pressure vessels.
Verified Ultimate Owner: Not publicly verified (Winston B. Young founded 1987; current beneficial ownership unconfirmed)
Operating Entity / Site Note: U.S. corporation & facilities; ownership control not publicly disclosed.
NAICS Assessment: ACCURATE - 332420; heavy-gauge pressure vessels are the physical product & central process.
Verification Notes / Uncertainty: Capability high-confidence (SAM + company site); beneficial ownership unverified.

Primary NAICS Description: Heating Equipment (except Warm Air Furnaces) Manufacturing
What They Make or Do: Vertically integrated maker of engineered electric-heating/thermal-management equipment: immersion heaters, circulation/in-line process heaters, process-air heaters, tubular elements, marine heaters, SCR/step controls, control panels; ASME pressure-vessel heater packages.
Primary Products or Capabilities: Immersion/process/marine electric heaters; tubular heating elements; SCR/step controls & panels.
Operating Entity / Site Note: U.S. entity; immediate legal holding between INDEECO and SPX not separately specified in cited materials.
NAICS Assessment: ACCURATE - 333414 'Heating Equipment Mfg' directly describes the output; heating flowing fluids does not make it a piping supplier.
Verification Notes / Uncertainty: Products/parent from INDEECO site, ASPEQ/SPX acquisition release, SPX SEC subsidiary exhibit.
